column1: The pirozhki is a well-known food around the world. It_____________comes from Russia. (ORIGINAL)
Pirozhkis are made using dough filled with meat and____________vegetables. (DIFFER)
They are then baked in the oven or fried until they become a __________brown colour. (GOLD)
The pirozhki has entered the cuisines of other countries, where it is made in_____________ways so that resembles local food. (VARY)
Most non-Russian people______________the word "pirozhki". The accent falls on the last syllable, not the middle. You're supposed to say "pee-ro-SHKEE". (PRONOUNCE)
Because pirizhki are made quite ___________, they are popular as "street food" in many places worldwide. (EASY)
Alan walked into a bank to speak to someone about his cash card. He was____________because the machine outside had just taken it from him. (HAPPY)
"Did you____________change your PIN number?" the woman in the bank asked Alan. (RECENT)
Alan thought for a moment. He had the______________that he had changed it. (FEEL)
"Yes, I think I tried to use the old number. I clearly didn't____________the new one well enough," said Alan (MEMORY)
Just then, a man walked up to the counter. "I found this card in the machine. Maybe it belongs to one of your_____________," he said. (CUSTOM)
It was Alan's card, and he thanked the man. The card was_____________, though, because Alan still couldn't remember the new PIN number. (USE)
